Data Cleaning

2026-04-26

library(tidyverse)
## ── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
## ✔ dplyr     1.2.0     ✔ readr     2.2.0
## ✔ forcats   1.0.1     ✔ stringr   1.6.0
## ✔ ggplot2   4.0.2     ✔ tibble    3.3.1
## ✔ lubridate 1.9.5     ✔ tidyr     1.3.2
## ✔ purrr     1.2.1     
## ── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
## ✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
## ✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
## ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors
data = read_csv("Raw Data.csv")
## New names:
## Rows: 792 Columns: 28
## ── Column specification
## ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Delimiter: "," chr
## (22): WorkerId, HITTypeId, CreationTime, AssignmentId, AcceptTime...7, S... dbl
## (6): Reward, MaxAssignments, WorkTimeInSeconds...9, Answer.surveycode, ...
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data. ℹ
## Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
## • `AcceptTime` -> `AcceptTime...7`
## • `WorkTimeInSeconds` -> `WorkTimeInSeconds...9`
## • `AcceptTime` -> `AcceptTime...16`
## • `WorkTimeInSeconds` -> `WorkTimeInSeconds...17`
## • `` -> `...27`
## • `` -> `...28`
# find size and missing value counts
data <- read_csv("Raw Data.csv", na = c("NA", "#N/A"))
## New names:
## Rows: 792 Columns: 28
## ── Column specification
## ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Delimiter: "," chr
## (17): WorkerId, HITTypeId, CreationTime, AssignmentId, AcceptTime...7, S... dbl
## (11): Reward, MaxAssignments, WorkTimeInSeconds...9, Answer.surveycode, ...
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data. ℹ
## Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
## • `AcceptTime` -> `AcceptTime...7`
## • `WorkTimeInSeconds` -> `WorkTimeInSeconds...9`
## • `AcceptTime` -> `AcceptTime...16`
## • `WorkTimeInSeconds` -> `WorkTimeInSeconds...17`
## • `` -> `...27`
## • `` -> `...28`
data |>
  summarise(across(everything(), ~ sum(is.na(.)))) |>
  pivot_longer(cols = everything(),
               names_to = "variable",
               values_to = "n_missing") |>
  mutate(pct_missing = n_missing / nrow(data) * 100) |>
  print(n = Inf)
## # A tibble: 28 × 3
##    variable                                                n_missing pct_missing
##    <chr>                                                       <int>       <dbl>
##  1 WorkerId                                                        0         0  
##  2 HITTypeId                                                       0         0  
##  3 Reward                                                          0         0  
##  4 CreationTime                                                    0         0  
##  5 MaxAssignments                                                  0         0  
##  6 AssignmentId                                                    0         0  
##  7 AcceptTime...7                                                  0         0  
##  8 SubmitTime                                                      0         0  
##  9 WorkTimeInSeconds...9                                           0         0  
## 10 Answer.surveycode                                               0         0  
## 11 multiplier                                                      0         0  
## 12 amount                                                          0         0  
## 13 age                                                             0         0  
## 14 gender                                                          0         0  
## 15 income                                                          0         0  
## 16 AcceptTime...16                                               271        34.2
## 17 WorkTimeInSeconds...17                                        271        34.2
## 18 IPAddress                                                     273        34.5
## 19 LocationLatitude                                              273        34.5
## 20 LocationLongitude                                             273        34.5
## 21 Q3_1 On a scale of 0 to 100, how would you describe yo…       273        34.5
## 22 Q8_1 On a scale of 0 to 100, how would you describe yo…       273        34.5
## 23 Q5 In the 2016 Presidential election, who did you vote…       273        34.5
## 24 Q9 What State do you live in?                                 273        34.5
## 25 Q7 Do you consider yourself a:                                273        34.5
## 26 Q8 Aside from weddings and funerals, how often do you …       273        34.5
## 27 ...27                                                           0         0  
## 28 ...28                                                           0         0
# check how many AssignmentId are there
data |>
  summarise(n_unique = n_distinct(AssignmentId))
## # A tibble: 1 × 1
##   n_unique
##      <int>
## 1      792
#AssignmentId has 792 unique ID, so it's also useless

Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

data |>
  summarise(n_unique = n_distinct(HITTypeId))
## # A tibble: 1 × 1
##   n_unique
##      <int>
## 1        1
# same, useless
data |>
  summarise(n_unique = n_distinct(Reward))
## # A tibble: 1 × 1
##   n_unique
##      <int>
## 1        1
# all of them are 0.25
unique(data$MaxAssignments)
## [1] 203 193 200 100 220 173
# [1] 203 193 200 100 220 173 maybe a batch indicator
unique(data$CreationTime)
## [1] "Tue Sep 25 12:15:58 PDT 2018" "Tue Sep 25 10:44:25 PDT 2018"
## [3] "Fri Sep 21 15:08:08 PDT 2018" "Fri Sep 21 11:31:30 PDT 2018"
## [5] "Fri Jun 22 14:01:52 PDT 2018" "Thu Jun 21 11:46:58 PDT 2018"
## [7] "Fri Jun 22 08:58:59 PDT 2018"
#[1] "Tue Sep 25 12:15:58 PDT 2018" "Tue Sep 25 10:44:25 PDT 2018" "Fri Sep 21 15:08:08 PDT 2018"
#[4] "Fri Sep 21 11:31:30 PDT 2018" "Fri Jun 22 14:01:52 PDT 2018" "Thu Jun 21 11:46:58 PDT 2018"
#[7] "Fri Jun 22 08:58:59 PDT 2018"

# 7 batches of questionaires categorical variable, use this as batch indicator,remove maxassignments
# change creation time to 7 categories
data <- data |>
  mutate(
    batch = factor(CreationTime),
    batch = factor(batch, labels = letters[1:nlevels(batch)])
  ) |>
  select(-CreationTime)
unique(data$AcceptTime...7)
##   [1] "Tue Sep 25 12:18:22 PDT 2018" "Tue Sep 25 12:18:51 PDT 2018"
##   [3] "Tue Sep 25 12:17:49 PDT 2018" "Tue Sep 25 12:16:28 PDT 2018"
##   [5] "Tue Sep 25 12:22:43 PDT 2018" "Tue Sep 25 12:19:22 PDT 2018"
##   [7] "Tue Sep 25 12:16:32 PDT 2018" "Tue Sep 25 12:16:39 PDT 2018"

##  [11] "Tue Sep 25 12:19:57 PDT 2018" "Tue Sep 25 12:20:13 PDT 2018"

# submit time has more than 600 unique values, useless

unique(data$Answer.surveycode)

## [637] 23839 44952 94622 44045 76447 40102  2997 19452   469 76129 67327 98679
## [649] 30053 74545 93971 33109 66424 25251 43227 98915 17353 72298 28898 15031
## [661] 52572 95840 15331 51858 90496 47111  6791   360 58752   172 81236 51428
## [673] 69491 47976 26221 11770 53869 69430 87828 15075 16651 60674 18372 73942
## [685] 88012 79712 58806 29300 64696 59063   882 58196 66170 88902 28980 63313
## [697] 28030  1216 15478 25375 47246 16192 89710 25379 70845  3984 58338 27866
## [709] 67701 88316 98321 30165 70126 92809 13623 74557 72383 58445  6953 61154
## [721] 29054 12108 72301 74182 83828 17379 75543  3041 15045 95854 42287  9904
## [733] 44082 60017  8767 30858 73633 55900 27323 11590 29642 21730 14677 75150
## [745] 23824 53495 37125 53848 90723 72420 59658  3112 37289 32313 65364 98997
## [757] 93403 13122 64160 31681 45458 62848 71826 85148 21925 27373 14197 61512
## [769] 14287 62744 48133 52608 88188 48014 32339 81637 77540 95373 34830 85893
## [781] 22735 77361 77677 12675 74142 71949
# useless everyone has a unique code
#remove 27,28,WorkerId,IPAddress,LocationLatitude,LocationLongitude,AssignmentId, etc
clean_data <- data |>
  select(-any_of(c(
    "...27", "...28",
    "WorkerId", "IPAddress",
    "LocationLatitude", "LocationLongitude",
    "AssignmentId","HITTypeId","Reward","AcceptTime...7", "AcceptTime...16","MaxAssignments","SubmitTime","Answer.surveycode"
    )))
# Time spent
library(ggplot2)
data |>
  ggplot(aes(x = `WorkTimeInSeconds...9`)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Work Time (Seconds)",
    x = "Time (seconds)",
    y = "Count"
  )

# how fast was the fastest person finish this questionaire
data |>
  summarise(min_time = min(`WorkTimeInSeconds...9`, na.rm = TRUE))
## # A tibble: 1 × 1
##   min_time
##      <dbl>
## 1        3
# 3 seconds. not a reliable observation

# how many people spent less than 10 seconds
data |>
  summarise(n_less_5 = sum(`WorkTimeInSeconds...9` < 10, na.rm = TRUE))
## # A tibble: 1 × 1
##   n_less_5
##      <int>
## 1        1
#only one. good. keep this variable as a reference
# variable multiplier
data |>
  ggplot(aes(x = multiplier)) +
  geom_bar() +
  labs(
    title = "Count of Each Multiplier Value",
    x = "Multiplier",
    y = "Count"
  )

# age
unique(data$age)
## [1] "60-69"               "18-29"               "30-39"              
## [4] "50-59"               "40-49"               "70 or over"         
## [7] "Prefer not to state"
#[1] "60-69"               "18-29"               "30-39"               "50-59"               "40-49"              
#[6] "70 or over"          "Prefer not to state"
clean_data |>
  count(gender)
## # A tibble: 3 × 2
##   gender                          n
##   <chr>                       <int>
## 1 Female                        373
## 2 Male                          411
## 3 Other / Prefer not to state     8
clean_data |>
  count(income)
## # A tibble: 7 × 2
##   income                  n
##   <chr>               <int>
## 1 $20,000 - $39,999     190
## 2 $40,000 - $59,999     177
## 3 $60,000 - $79,999     130
## 4 $80,000 - $99,999      81
## 5 Over $100,000         102
## 6 Prefer not to state    11
## 7 Under $20,000         101
library(ggplot2)

clean_data |>
  ggplot(aes(
    x = `Q8_1 On a scale of 0 to 100, how would you describe your religious orientation?`,
    y = amount
  )) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(
    title = "Amount vs Religious Orientation",
    x = "Religious Orientation (0–100)",
    y = "Amount Donated"
  )
## Warning: Removed 273 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_point()`).

model1 <- clean_data |>
  lm(amount ~ `Q8_1 On a scale of 0 to 100, how would you describe your religious orientation?`, data = _)

summary(model1)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = amount ~ `Q8_1 On a scale of 0 to 100, how would you describe your religious orientation?`, 
##     data = clean_data)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -0.4924 -0.2557 -0.1007  0.2159  0.7443 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                                                                                    Estimate
## (Intercept)                                                                       0.2557211
## `Q8_1 On a scale of 0 to 100, how would you describe your religious orientation?` 0.0023664
##                                                                                   Std. Error
## (Intercept)                                                                        0.0214229
## `Q8_1 On a scale of 0 to 100, how would you describe your religious orientation?`  0.0004355
##                                                                                   t value
## (Intercept)                                                                        11.937
## `Q8_1 On a scale of 0 to 100, how would you describe your religious orientation?`   5.434
##                                                                                   Pr(>|t|)
## (Intercept)                                                                        < 2e-16
## `Q8_1 On a scale of 0 to 100, how would you describe your religious orientation?` 8.52e-08
##                                                                                      
## (Intercept)                                                                       ***
## `Q8_1 On a scale of 0 to 100, how would you describe your religious orientation?` ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 0.3565 on 517 degrees of freedom
##   (273 observations deleted due to missingness)
## Multiple R-squared:  0.05402,    Adjusted R-squared:  0.05219 
## F-statistic: 29.52 on 1 and 517 DF,  p-value: 8.524e-08
clean_data |>
  ggplot(aes(
    x = `Q8_1 On a scale of 0 to 100, how would you describe your religious orientation?`,
    y = amount
  )) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.4) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm") +
  labs(
    title = "Linear Fit: Amount vs Religiosity",
    x = "Religiosity (0–100)",
    y = "Amount Donated"
  )
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
## Warning: Removed 273 rows containing non-finite outside the scale range
## (`stat_smooth()`).
## Warning: Removed 273 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_point()`).

clean_data |>
  ggplot(aes(
    x = `Q3_1 On a scale of 0 to 100, how would you describe your political views?`,
    y = amount
  )) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(
    title = "Amount vs political views",
    x = "Religious Orientation (0–100)",
    y = "Amount Donated"
  )
## Warning: Removed 273 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_point()`).

model2 <- clean_data |>
  lm(amount ~ `Q3_1 On a scale of 0 to 100, how would you describe your political views?`, data = _)

summary(model2)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = amount ~ `Q3_1 On a scale of 0 to 100, how would you describe your political views?`, 
##     data = clean_data)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -0.3696 -0.3261 -0.1261  0.2374  0.6841 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                                                                               Estimate
## (Intercept)                                                                  0.3696103
## `Q3_1 On a scale of 0 to 100, how would you describe your political views?` -0.0005372
##                                                                             Std. Error
## (Intercept)                                                                  0.0386549
## `Q3_1 On a scale of 0 to 100, how would you describe your political views?`  0.0005490
##                                                                             t value
## (Intercept)                                                                   9.562
## `Q3_1 On a scale of 0 to 100, how would you describe your political views?`  -0.979
##                                                                             Pr(>|t|)
## (Intercept)                                                                   <2e-16
## `Q3_1 On a scale of 0 to 100, how would you describe your political views?`    0.328
##                                                                                
## (Intercept)                                                                 ***
## `Q3_1 On a scale of 0 to 100, how would you describe your political views?`    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 0.3662 on 517 degrees of freedom
##   (273 observations deleted due to missingness)
## Multiple R-squared:  0.001849,   Adjusted R-squared:  -8.208e-05 
## F-statistic: 0.9575 on 1 and 517 DF,  p-value: 0.3283
clean_data |>
  ggplot(aes(
    x = `Q3_1 On a scale of 0 to 100, how would you describe your political views?`,
    y = amount
  )) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.4) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm") +
  labs(
    title = "Linear Fit: Amount vs political views",
    x = "political views (0–100)",
    y = "Amount Donated"
  )
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
## Warning: Removed 273 rows containing non-finite outside the scale range
## (`stat_smooth()`).
## Warning: Removed 273 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_point()`).

clean_data |>
  ggplot(aes(
    x = `Q8 Aside from weddings and funerals, how often do you attend religious services?`,
    y = amount
  )) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(
    title = "Amount vs political views",
    x = "Religious services frequency",
    y = "Amount Donated"
  )

model3 <- clean_data |>
  lm(amount ~ `Q8 Aside from weddings and funerals, how often do you attend religious services?`, data = _)

summary(model2)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = amount ~ `Q3_1 On a scale of 0 to 100, how would you describe your political views?`, 
##     data = clean_data)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -0.3696 -0.3261 -0.1261  0.2374  0.6841 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                                                                               Estimate
## (Intercept)                                                                  0.3696103
## `Q3_1 On a scale of 0 to 100, how would you describe your political views?` -0.0005372
##                                                                             Std. Error
## (Intercept)                                                                  0.0386549
## `Q3_1 On a scale of 0 to 100, how would you describe your political views?`  0.0005490
##                                                                             t value
## (Intercept)                                                                   9.562
## `Q3_1 On a scale of 0 to 100, how would you describe your political views?`  -0.979
##                                                                             Pr(>|t|)
## (Intercept)                                                                   <2e-16
## `Q3_1 On a scale of 0 to 100, how would you describe your political views?`    0.328
##                                                                                
## (Intercept)                                                                 ***
## `Q3_1 On a scale of 0 to 100, how would you describe your political views?`    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 0.3662 on 517 degrees of freedom
##   (273 observations deleted due to missingness)
## Multiple R-squared:  0.001849,   Adjusted R-squared:  -8.208e-05 
## F-statistic: 0.9575 on 1 and 517 DF,  p-value: 0.3283
clean_data |>
  ggplot(aes(
    x = `Q3_1 On a scale of 0 to 100, how would you describe your political views?`,
    y = amount
  )) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.4) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm") +
  labs(
    title = "Linear Fit: Amount vs Religious services frequency",
    x = "Religious services frequency",
    y = "Amount Donated"
  )
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
## Warning: Removed 273 rows containing non-finite outside the scale range
## (`stat_smooth()`).
## Warning: Removed 273 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range
## (`geom_point()`).

# missing values
clean_data |>
  summarise(across(everything(), ~ sum(is.na(.)))) |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols = everything(),
    names_to = "variable",
    values_to = "n_missing"
  ) |>
  mutate(pct_missing = n_missing / nrow(clean_data) * 100) |>
  arrange(desc(n_missing)) |>
  print(n = Inf)
## # A tibble: 14 × 3
##    variable                                                n_missing pct_missing
##    <chr>                                                       <int>       <dbl>
##  1 Q3_1 On a scale of 0 to 100, how would you describe yo…       273        34.5
##  2 Q8_1 On a scale of 0 to 100, how would you describe yo…       273        34.5
##  3 Q5 In the 2016 Presidential election, who did you vote…       273        34.5
##  4 Q9 What State do you live in?                                 273        34.5
##  5 Q7 Do you consider yourself a:                                273        34.5
##  6 Q8 Aside from weddings and funerals, how often do you …       273        34.5
##  7 WorkTimeInSeconds...17                                        271        34.2
##  8 WorkTimeInSeconds...9                                           0         0  
##  9 multiplier                                                      0         0  
## 10 amount                                                          0         0  
## 11 age                                                             0         0  
## 12 gender                                                          0         0  
## 13 income                                                          0         0  
## 14 batch                                                           0         0
library(readr)

write_csv(clean_data, "clean_data.csv")
